title: Turbidity NTU Optical Sensor Module
design_question: How to measure water clarity (turbidity) in NTU units for a portable water quality monitor?
selected_approach: DFRobot SEN0189 turbidity sensor (0–3000 NTU, 5 V supply, analog voltage output 0–4.5 V). Immersed directly in water sample. MCU reads on 10-bit ADC (5 V reference): NTU = −1120.4 × V² + 5742.3 × V − 4353.8 (DFRobot calibration curve polynomial). Two-point calibration with distilled water (0 NTU) and standard formazin (100 NTU) solution.
rationale: Turbidity is one of the most accessible and ecologically relevant water quality parameters. DFRobot SEN0189 requires no special reagents and returns a continuous analog voltage. 3000 NTU range covers clean tap water (< 1 NTU) to highly turbid river/runoff water (1000+ NTU). Calibration polynomial verified against DFRobot wiki; stable within ±5 % per calibration cycle.

alternatives.reason_rejected: More accurate at low NTU (ISO 7027 compliant) but requires specific geometry, sealed housing, and clean LED wavelength control — complexity not justified for portable monitor.

alternatives.approach: DIY LED + LDR in water-tight housing
alternatives.reason_rejected: Requires full custom construction and manual calibration; not reproducible without calibrated reference solutions.

verification_notes: FACT: 'Turbidity Sensing — NTU Optical Measurement' confirms NTU units, sensor placement requirements, and two-point calibration with formazin standards.
